title: Bridge Essay: Psilocybin induces rapid and persistent growth of dendritic spines in frontal co

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nonordinary Rendering** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nonordinary — psychedelic decoupling, ego dissolution, DMN disruption, altered states.

**Psilocybin induces rapid and persistent growth of dendritic spines in frontal cortex in vivo.**. Neuron.. PMID: 34228959. DOI: 10.1016/j.neuron.2021.06.008. Psilocybin is aserotonergic psychedelic with untapped therapeutic potential. There are hints that the use of psychedelics can produce neural adaptations, although the extent and timescale of the impact in amammalian brain are unknown. In this study, we used chronic two-photon microscopy to image longitudinally the apical dendritic spines of layer 5 pyramidal neurons in the mouse medial frontal cortex.
